game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: Geometry Dash Geometry Dash is a rhythm-based platformer where players control a geometric icon, tapping the screen to jump, fly, or flip, navigating a series of obstacles and spikes synchronized to music in auto-scrolling levels. The game's core mechanics involve precise timing and adapting to different game modes (e.g., cube, ship, ball) that alter player movement, with death resulting in a restart from the beginning or a last checkpoint in practice mode.
game_info.description: Navigate your geometric cube through a challenging auto-scrolling level filled with spikes, platforms, and obstacles. Time your jumps perfectly to avoid hazards and reach the finish line. The game speeds up as you progress, requiring increasingly precise timing. Complete the level to win!
game_info.controls:
SPACE: Jump (tap to hop over obstacles)
ENTER: Start game from title screen
ESC: Pause/Unpause game
R: Restart game (when game over or at any time)
game_info.automated_testing:
<TEST_1>
<test_description>Tests that the game compiles without errors and can be played with sticky keys, ensuring basic functionality and responsiveness.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Alternates between jumping and not jumping at semi-random intervals to navigate through the level and respond to obstacles.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The game runs without errors and the cube responds to inputs, demonstrating the game is functional and playable.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_1>

<TEST_2>
<test_description>Tests that the game can be won by implementing optimal jumping strategies.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Uses predictive jumping based on upcoming obstacle positions, calculating the perfect timing to clear all hazards.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The cube successfully reaches the end of the level, triggering the win condition and GAME_OVER_WIN phase.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_2>

<TEST_3>
<test_description>Tests the game's difficulty progression by measuring survival time at different stages.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Uses basic obstacle avoidance with timing adjustments for increased game speed in later sections.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The test demonstrates increasing difficulty through survival metrics, with harder sections requiring more precise timing.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_3>

<TEST_4>
<test_description>Tests collision detection accuracy with obstacles of different sizes and positions.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Performs precise jumps near obstacle edges to verify collision boundaries are accurate and fair.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Collisions are correctly detected only when the player model actually touches obstacles, with no false positives or negatives.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_4>

<TEST_5>
<test_description>Tests the game's checkpoint system by deliberately dying after passing checkpoints.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Navigates to checkpoints and then intentionally collides with obstacles to verify proper respawn mechanics.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The player correctly respawns at the most recent checkpoint rather than the beginning of the level.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_5>
